system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message: Quick travel question. I'm a US passport holder heading to Vietnam in two weeks for a 12-day trip — Hanoi and Ha Long Bay, just tourism. Do I need a visa for Vietnam? My passport is valid for another 3 years.
turn2_user_message: Perfect, thanks. Can you write me a quick trip-prep checklist for the departure airport — what documents to bring, what to expect at check-in and at Hanoi immigration, and anything I should print? I travel light and want to make sure I'm not missing anything.
response_turn1:
Good news! As a US passport holder, you **do NOT need a visa** for your 12-day trip to Vietnam.

**Key details:**
- Vietnam offers **visa-free entry for US citizens for up to 45 days** for tourism purposes
- Your passport validity (3 years remaining) exceeds the typical 6-month requirement, so you're all set there
- This applies to single-entry visits

**What you need:**
- Valid US passport
- Proof of onward/return travel (flight ticket out of Vietnam)
- That's it!

Just make sure you have your return flight confirmation handy when you arrive, as immigration may ask to see it. You'll simply get an entry stamp upon arrival at the airport in Hanoi.

Have a great trip exploring Hanoi and Ha Long Bay! The visa exemption makes it very straightforward for US travelers now.
response_turn2:
# Vietnam Trip Departure & Arrival Checklist

## 📋 Documents to Bring

**Essential (keep in carry-on):**
- [ ] Valid US passport (check expiration date one more time)
- [ ] Return/onward flight confirmation
- [ ] Accommodation details (hotel names/addresses)
- [ ] Travel insurance policy (if you have it)
- [ ] Credit cards + some USD cash for emergencies

**Recommended to print (or have digital backup):**
- [ ] Flight confirmation/boarding pass
- [ ] First night's hotel reservation
- [ ] Emergency contacts list

## ✈️ At US Departure - Check-In

**What to expect:**
- Airline will verify your passport is valid
- They may ask to see proof of onward travel (return ticket)
- Standard security screening
- No Vietnam visa questions since US citizens are visa-exempt

## 🛬 At Hanoi Immigration (Noi Bai Airport)

**What to expect:**
1. **Arrival card:** You'll fill out an arrival/departure card on the plane (pen handy!)
2. **Immigration queue:** Look for "Foreigners/All Passports" line
3. **Immigration officer will:**
   - Check your passport
   - May ask purpose of visit (say "tourism")
   - May ask how long you're staying (say "12 days")
   - May ask to see return flight confirmation
   - Stamp your passport with 45-day visa exemption
4. **Baggage claim & customs**

**Pro tips:**
- Have your return flight details easily accessible (screenshot is fine)
- Know your first hotel's name and address
- Process typically takes 15-45 minutes depending on crowds
- Have a pen for the arrival card

## 💡 Travel Light Essentials

Since you're traveling light:
- Phone with screenshots of all key documents
- Physical passport (obviously!)
- One printed copy of return flight as backup
- That's really all you *need* document-wise

**Don't forget:**
- Vietnam uses 220V power (bring adapter if needed)
- Local SIM card or international phone plan
- Small amount of Vietnamese Dong or USD for arrival (taxi, tips)

You're all set! Immigration in Hanoi is straightforward for US tourists. Safe travels!
